title.en: ISO Invariance — Push in RAW vs Crank the ISO
title.ko: ISO 무변성 — RAW push vs ISO 올리기

subtitle.en: on an ISO-invariant sensor, shooting underexposed at ISO 100 and pushing the RAW +5 stops is as good as shooting at ISO 3200; on a non-invariant sensor, the push wins or loses depending on where read noise enters
subtitle.ko: ISO-invariant 센서: ISO 100에서 −5 EV + RAW push +5 = ISO 3200 정상 노출. Non-invariant 센서: 결과 다름

description.en: Two paths: ISO 100 underexposed + RAW push vs ISO 3200 correct exposure. Invariant sensor: identical noise. Non-invariant sensor: post-gain read noise pushed by the digital boost, Path A loses.
description.ko: 두 경로: ISO 100 노출부족 + RAW push vs ISO 3200 정상. Invariant: 같은 노이즈. Non-invariant: 후단 read noise가 디지털 push로 증폭, Path A 불리.
